plant.description: Unique History: Mentioned in the Charaka Samhita as the best among 'Sangrahi' (absorbent) pulses. Historically used as a primary protein source for scholars and high-performance individuals due to its 'Ojas' enhancing quality. Regarded as 'Mother of Complexion'. Physical Characteristics: A small, erect, annual herb reaching up to 0.3-0.5 meters in height, with quadrangular stems and opposite, pinnate (leaflets) leaves with small, white-to-pale blue flowers. The fruit consists of small, inflated pods containing 1-2 small, lens-shaped, reddish-orange-to-brown seeds. Found throughout India and tropical regions worldwide, cultivated extensively for its seeds. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Proanthocyanidins, Saponins, and diversos Protease inhibitors. The presence of these compounds is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-tumor, and anti-inflammatory action on the heart and skin cells. Strict Standardizations: The seed is standardized in API based on its reddish-orange-to-brown color and characteristic sweet-mild-nutty taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: moisture content (<12%), protein content (>24%).

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful Pitta and Kapha Hara (pacifies Pitta and Kapha doshas). Due to its Madhura (sweet) and Kashaya (astringent) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious pulse for 'Sangrahi' (absorbent) and 'Varnya' (complexion-improving) therapies. While it is cooling and light (Laghu), it is 'Ruksha' (dry), making it a Vata-increasing grain (Vatala) if not cooked with unctuous substances.

masura_yusha__medicated_soup_.method: 1 part seeds boiled with 14 parts water, seasoned with ginger and cumin. Take as a primary meal for managing diarrhea and skin issues.

masura_lepa__face_mask_.method: Seed powder mixed with milk or rose water; applied to the face for 20 minutes to treat acne and enhance radiance.

odana__cooked_pulse_.method: Cooked with minimal water and seasoned with digestive spices like pepper and hing to counter the Vata-increasing effect.

saktu_powder.method: Roasted masura seeds ground into fine powder; taken with water as an instant energy drink during summer.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 10 - 20 grams of cooked pulse mixed with ghee once twice for muscle development and skin issues during childhood infections.
dosage.adults: Daily staple food (50-100g of cooked pulse per meal).
dosage.elderly: Highly recommended to prevent age-related Pitta issues and maintain skin radiance. Dosage: 50g of cooked pulse daily.

medicinal_uses: Expert Skin Health and Complexion Specialist (Varnya): Masura is the premier Ayurvedic pulse for facial radiance and managing acne. Its 'Sheeta' (cooling) and 'Kashaya' (astringent) nature helps in absorbing systemic Pitta and providing systemic stabilization of the 'Bhrajaka-Pitta' (skin-fire). WHO-aligned research confirms its high digestibility and rich antioxidant content.

Gastrointestinal and Diarrhea Care Specialist (Sangrahi): Historically used as 'Masura-Yusha' (soup) for managing burning diarrhea and dysentery. Its 'Grahi' (absorbent) and 'Kashaya' (astringent) actions help in strengthening the gut lining and binding the stool without causing heaviness.

Internal Hemorrhage and Bleeding (Raktapitta): Recognized as 'Raktapitta-hara' (bleeding-destroyer). It effectively cools the blood and stops internal bleeding from the lungs, stomach, and pelvic organs by providing systemic cooling and providing systemic hemostatic balance.

Anti-inflammatory and Fever Relief: Its cooling potency (Sheeta) provides instant relief from internal burning sensations (Daha), sunstrokes, and inflammatory fevers. It helps in Reducing systemic heat and inflammatory congestion.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Proteins (24-26%), Carbohydrates, Flavonoids (Vitexin, Isovitexin), and Essential Minerals (Iron, Zinc). Vitexin and Isovitexin are the primary bioactives responsible for its potent anti-inflammatory and cardio-protective properties.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe staple food with a history of documented safety for thousands of years.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Vata (severe constipation, bloating, dry skin) should use it in moderation and always cook with unctuous substances like ghee. Avoid during extreme dehydration due to its 'Ruksha' (dry) nature.
possible_side_effects: Overconsumption leads to excessive gas and bloating if not appropriately cooked.
drug_interactions: None documented. It acts as a neutral carrier for many medicines.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
